title: Zero modes of non-abelian Dirac operator in topologically non-trivial band insulator
abstract: We show that the local gauge-invariance of the quantum geometric tensor (QGT) defined in the Block-momentum space of a generic $N$-level (sublattice degrees of freedom) band insulator implies the existence of zero modes of non-abelian Dirac operator in such momentum space. Solutions of these zero modes equations in the two-dimensional Brillouin zone torus, in terms of Jacobi Theta function determine the probability amplitudes associated with the $N$-component ground state wave-function under adiabatic approximation in this Hilbert space. These solutions subjected to normalization, defines a complex projective ($CP$) space of $N-1$ dimension ($CP^{N-1}$ space) when one or more degeneracy points exist in the dispersion spectrum of such band-isulator. We show how the non-abelian generalization of the vortexability criterion of Chern bands automatically follows from these zero-mode equations, and also demonstrate their connection with momentum space-version of Lowest landau level algebra. Subsequently we write an Euclidean action from which these zero mode equations follow. We point out that the non-interacting part of different paradigms used to understand fractional Chern insulator(FCI) like phases in a host of two-dimensional material can be understood within this approach. We analyse two effective hamiltonian : lattice Dirac (QZW) model and two-band model for rhombohedral $N$-layer graphene in our propsoed framework and obtain important conclusions.
summary: This paper establishes that zero modes of a non-abelian Dirac operator are a generic feature of topologically non-trivial band insulators. By linking the geometry of the Quantum Geometric Tensor to these zero modes, the authors provide a unified non-interacting foundation for understanding complex phases like Fractional Chern Insulators.
main_problem: Investigating the existence and implications of zero modes of a non-abelian Dirac operator in the momentum space of topologically non-trivial band insulators.
main_result: The authors prove that the local gauge-invariance of the Quantum Geometric Tensor (QGT) implies the existence of zero modes for a non-abelian Dirac operator, providing a theoretical framework for understanding Fractional Chern Insulator (FCI) phases.
method: The study utilizes non-abelian gauge theory, the quantum adiabatic approximation, and the minimization of a Euclidean action, with explicit solutions for the zero modes expressed via Jacobi Theta functions.
model_or_system: The framework applies to generic N-level (sublattice/orbital) band insulators with degeneracy points in the Brillouin zone, specifically analyzing the lattice Dirac (QWZ) model and rhombohedral N-layer graphene.
key_observables: Quantum Geometric Tensor (QGT), non-abelian Berry connection (Wilczek-Zee connection), Berry curvature, and Fubini-Study metric.
important_parameters: Number of bands (N), displacement field energy (D), generalized Dirac velocity, and the presence of degeneracy points in the Brillouin zone.
main_assumptions: The system is assumed to be in the quantum adiabatic limit, and the derivation relies on the local gauge-invariance of the QGT.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows dispersion plots for the lattice Dirac model and continuum dispersion for rhombohedral multilayer graphene; Figure 2 shows the reciprocal lattice of the lattice Dirac model.
paper_structure: The paper begins by deriving the gauge-invariant QGT and zero-mode equations, moves to solving these equations using holomorphic representations and Jacobi Theta functions, applies the framework to specific models (QWZ and rhombohedral graphene), and concludes with a mathematical appendix on theta function properties.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
